
---
title: "Used Car Price Prediction – Model Comparison"
date: "2025-10-1"
categories: [Regression]
image: "01car.jpg"
image-alt: "image of used cars"
description: "Predicting the price of used cars based on various attributes and evaluating with RMSE"
---

::: {.callout-important}
The Project 100% completed and fully functional but the
detailed case study and walkthrough on this Quarto site are being finalized — coming in the next 1–2 days.
:::


## Project Overview

This project is based on a [competition on kaggle](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/playground-series-s4e9).






## Objective
Build an accurate pricing model for second-hand cars to support dealers and marketplaces.

## Dataset
~25,000 used car listings with features:  
brand, model, year, mileage, fuel type, transmission, engine size, location, etc.

## Model Comparison (Test Set)

| Model               | R²     | RMSE | Training Time |
|---------------------|--------|--------------------|---------------|
| Linear Regression   | 0.72   | 28.4               | <1 s          |
| Random Forest       | 0.91   | 15.1               | 8 s           |
| XGBoost             | **0.94** | **12.8**         | 12 s          |
| LightGBM            | 0.93   | 13.5               | 3 s           |

**Winner: XGBoost** – best accuracy while remaining interpretable.

![Feature Importance (XGBoost)](images/car_price_importance.png)
![Actual vs Predicted Prices](images/car_price_actual_vs_pred.png)

**Tech Stack:** 
- Python 
- Pandas 
- Scikit-learn
- XGBoost 

::: {.iframe-container}
<iframe src="https://usedcarpredapp-bhwgx6h2tvu5cf48ue4pu3.streamlit.app//?embed=true" 
        width="100%" height="700" frameborder="0">
</iframe>
:::
